div.head: 149. Memorandum From Secretary of Defense Brown to the President’s Assistant for National Security Affairs (Brzezinski)Source: Carter Library, National Security Affairs, Brzezinski Material, Subject File, Box 6, Comprehensive Test Ban (CTB), 3–12/77. Secret.

list.head: SUBJECT
list.item: Conversation with The President on CTB Considerations

div.p: At your suggestion, I conveyed my misgivings about the peaceful nuclear explosions loophole in CTB to the President in a conversation yesterday.No record of this conversation has been found. According to the President’s Daily Diary, Carter and Brown met from 4:45 to 5:20 p.m. on May 4. (Carter Library, Presidential Materials) Moreover, I described the JCS position that a ban on nuclear testing would be contrary to our national security interests. I assured him that I would be working on the broadest, most balanced view possible here in the Defense Department.
The President agreed that CTB would be a useful topic for a luncheon discussion by him with the Joint Chiefs, Charles Duncan, you and me.

signed.note: Brown signed “Harold” at the end of the memorandum.
